description: Stonewall launched a campaign to end the ban on lesbian, gay and bi people serving in the armed forces.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Supreme Court of Canada reads sexual orientation into the Charter
description: The Supreme Court of Canada rules that sexual orientation should be read into the equality guarantee of the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, extending its protection to lesbians and gay men.

sources.label: UFCW Canada — LGBTQ History Timeline
queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Swiss activist federation HACH dissolves in favor of Pink Cross
description: The umbrella of Swiss gay activist working groups, HACH, dissolved itself; the national association Pink Cross carried on as its successor organization.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: The Human Rights Campaign broadens its name and mission
description: In 1995 the Human Rights Campaign dropped the word Fund from its name and expanded its mission statement to advocate equality for gay, lesbian, bisexual and transgender Americans at home, at work and in every community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Kings Cross Steelers form as the world's first gay rugby club
description: The Kings Cross Steelers were established in London in 1995 as the first rugby club in the world founded by and for gay men, opening team sport to openly gay players.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Mermaids support group is formed
description: Mermaids, a support group for trans and gender-diverse children and their families, was founded in the United Kingdom.

sources.label: Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline

title: Triple combination therapy proves effective against HIV
description: In 1995 researchers showed that triple combination therapy using drugs such as 3TC, AZT and ddC could effectively treat HIV. The breakthrough transformed AIDS from a near-certain death sentence into a manageable condition for many patients.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: TUC and Stonewall launch For Richer or Poorer campaign
description: The TUC and Stonewall launched the For Richer or Poorer leaflet and lobbied for equal marriage for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Petition for equal rights for same-sex couples delivered with 85,181 signatures
description: Campaigners handed the petition 'Gleiche Rechte für gleichgeschlechtliche Paare' (Equal rights for same-sex couples), signed by 85,181 people, to the Federal Assembly at the Federal Palace in Bern. The petition became a key driver of the later registered-partnership legislation.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Rights commission finds Hamilton mayor discriminated
description: The Ontario Human Rights Commission found that the mayor of Hamilton had discriminated against homosexuals, in a case tied to the refusal to proclaim Pride.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Community center Uferlos opens in Lucerne
description: Uferlos, a new community center for gays and lesbians, opened its doors in Lucerne on 1 April 1995.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Ontario becomes first province to allow adoption
description: Ontario became the first Canadian province to allow same-sex couples to jointly adopt children.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: New CSD association stages a Gay Parade in Zurich
description: The newly formed CSD organizing association in Zurich held a Gay Parade on 24 June 1995, continuing the revival of the city's pride events.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: First public blessing ceremony for a gay couple held in Bern
description: Switzerland's first public church blessing of a gay couple took place in Bern on 8 July 1995.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Uferlos culture weeks bring queer arts festival to Bern
description: Gay and lesbian culture and festival weeks ran under the name Uferlos on the banks of the Aare in Bern from late July to mid-August 1995.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Network, an association of gay professionals, founded in Switzerland
description: Network, a Swiss association for gay men in leadership and professional positions, held its founding on 16 September 1995.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: London Pride refusal later ruled discriminatory
description: The City of London refused a request to proclaim Pride; the refusal was subsequently found to be discriminatory.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Hate crime sentencing law adds sexual orientation
description: The Hate Crimes Sentencing Enhancement Act took effect, letting judges impose harsher sentences when a victim was targeted because of characteristics including sexual orientation.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Burkina Faso equalizes its age of consent
description: Burkina Faso set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity in 1996 with the adoption of its new penal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Club Aera opens in Zurich
description: The Aera club opened in Zurich, initially without a license and legally from 1998. For years its Saturday nights were reserved for gay men under the motto 'No dick - no entry'.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Congressman Jim Kolbe comes out
description: Arizona Congressman Jim Kolbe came out as gay while serving in the US House, becoming an openly gay Republican member of Congress.

title: David Parks elected in Nevada
description: David Parks was elected to the Nevada Legislature, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ person to serve in the state's elected office.

title: European court protects a trans employee from bias
description: In P v S and Cornwall County Council the European Court of Justice ruled that dismissing an employee for being trans was unlawful sex discrimination, a landmark for trans workplace rights.

title: First Mister Gay Switzerland elected
description: Switzerland elected its first Mister Gay Switzerland, a title intended to give the gay community a public representative.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: HAZ over-25 group takes the name Spätzünder
description: The HAZ group for men over 25 in Zurich renamed itself 'Spätzünder' (late bloomers).

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 1990-1999

title: Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy enters early development
description: Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy (HAART), combining antiretroviral drugs including protease inhibitors to suppress HIV replication, entered early development. It would transform the prognosis of HIV and AIDS, and within a year could be taken as a single daily tablet.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Hungary recognizes unregistered cohabitation for same-sex couples
description: Hungary extended unregistered-cohabitation recognition to same-sex couples in 1996, granting them common-law partner status. The arrangement was replaced by registered partnerships in 2009.

title: Macau decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Macau's new penal code of 1996, adopted while the territory was under Portuguese administration, dropped criminal sanctions for consensual same-sex activity.

description: Professional golfer Muffin Spencer-Devlin came out in 1996, becoming the first LPGA player to do so publicly.

title: North Macedonia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 1996 the Republic of Macedonia (today North Macedonia) removed consensual same-sex activity from its criminal code.

description: Romania amended Article 200 of its penal code in 1996, ending criminal punishment for consensual same-sex acts conducted in private. Public expression remained penalized until the article's full repeal in 2001.

description: Sabrina Sojourner was elected as a shadow representative for the District of Columbia, becoming one of the first openly lesbian Black women elected to office in the United States.

description: The ABC drama Relativity aired the first open-mouth kiss between two women shown on American prime-time television in 1997.

description: The Gay and Lesbian Medical Association started the Journal of the Gay and Lesbian Medical Association in 1997, the first peer-reviewed multi-disciplinary journal devoted to LGBT health.

title: South Africa's final constitution takes effect with LGBTQ protection
description: South Africa's post-apartheid constitution came into force in February 1997, keeping sexual orientation as an explicitly protected ground in its equality clause — the first national constitution in the world to do so.

description: China set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

title: Ellen DeGeneres comes out, on and off screen
description: In April 1997 Ellen DeGeneres came out publicly as a lesbian, among the first major US celebrities to do so. Her sitcom character Ellen Morgan came out later that year, making her the first openly lesbian actress playing an openly lesbian lead on television.

title: Matthew Shepard fatally beaten near Laramie
description: Matthew Shepard was tied to a fence and severely beaten near Laramie, Wyoming, and died of his injuries days later; the killing drew national attention to anti-gay violence.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Shepard’s attackers make first court appearance
description: Russell Henderson and Aaron McKinney made their first court appearance after their arrest in the attack on Matthew Shepard; each later received two life sentences.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Rita Hester is murdered in Massachusetts
description: Rita Hester, a Black transgender woman, was stabbed to death in her apartment in Allston, Massachusetts, in November 1998. Her killing inspired the founding of the Transgender Day of Remembrance the following year.

title: Zurich voters approve city funding for sex-worker service Hermann
description: In a municipal ballot on 28 and 29 November 1998, Zurich voters granted 30,000 francs to Hermann, a counseling service for sex workers, clearly defeating the SVP campaign against the subsidy.

description: The EuroGames, a European sports event for queer people, took place in Zurich from 1 to 4 June 2000, framed by a cultural program named Warmer Mai. The organizers brought the Thurgau film festival Pink Apple to Zurich for the occasion, and it afterwards ran annually in both cities.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

description: On 6 September 2000 the association Warmer Mai was set up in Zurich; it went on to assemble a queer cultural program every May.

description: The Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras was formally recognised as a hallmark event.

title: Western Australia equalizes the age of consent
description: The Australian state of Western Australia set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Youth groups found DIAGONAL as national umbrella organization
description: The youth groups Spot25 (Zurich), Rose (Basel-Landschaft), ComingInn (Bern) and Whynot (Lucerne) create DIAGONAL, a national umbrella body for lesbian, bi and gay youth groups that also offers online counseling for young people.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Zurich campaign association forms to back the partnership law
description: Gay and lesbian organizations in Zurich join forces in the association "Ja zum Partnerschaftsgesetz" to campaign for the partnership legislation.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Hungary equalizes the age of consent
description: Hungary set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

title: Estonia equalizes the age of consent
description: Estonia set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Zurich cantonal parliament passes same-sex partnership act
description: The cantonal parliament of Zurich adopts a progressive partnership act for same-sex couples, clearing the way for a cantonal popular vote on the law later that year.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Northwest Territories first to protect gender identity
description: The Northwest Territories became the first jurisdiction in Canada to explicitly add gender identity to its human rights legislation.

title: HAZ launches discussion group for bisexual men
description: The Zurich organization HAZ (Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich) starts a discussion group for bisexual men, adding a dedicated bi space to its program.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Openly gay politician Pim Fortuyn assassinated
description: Dutch politician Pim Fortuyn, openly gay and leading his own party into national elections, was shot dead by Volkert van der Graaf in Hilversum shortly before the general election.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Court affirms Marc Hall's right to attend prom
description: The Ontario Superior Court ruled that student Marc Hall had the right to bring his boyfriend to his Catholic school prom.

title: Sweden legalizes adoption by same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to jointly adopt children in Sweden.

description: Austria set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

title: Canada extends immigration rights to same-sex partners
description: Canada extended immigration sponsorship rights to same-sex partners.

title: Zurich holds CSD under the motto We are family
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day parade takes place with the motto "We are family", bringing the annual demonstration through the city once more.

title: Cyprus equalizes the age of consent
description: Cyprus set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

title: Ontario court rules the marriage ban unconstitutional
description: The Ontario Superior Court ruled that prohibiting same-sex couples from marrying is unconstitutional, a decision central to the Halpern case.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Neuchâtel during Expo 02
description: Neuchâtel hosts a Lesbian & Gay Pride on the occasion of Expo 02, the Swiss national exhibition, bringing the march to the exhibition city.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Mongolia equalizes the age of consent
description: Mongolia set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Star march converges on the center of the canton of Zurich before partnership vote
description: Gay and lesbian demonstrators stage a first star march to the geographic midpoint of the canton of Zurich, campaigning for the cantonal partnership act put to voters on 22 September.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: South Africa legalizes adoption by same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to jointly adopt children in South Africa.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline
Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zurich voters approve same-sex partnership law in world-first referendum
description: Voters in the canton of Zurich accept the law on equal rights for same-sex partnerships with 62.7 percent in favor. It is the first time anywhere that such a law is created by popular vote.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000
Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Exhibition on lesbian and gay history opens at Zurich city hall
description: The exhibition "Unverschämt", on the past and present of lesbians and gay men, opens at the Stadthaus in Zurich. It runs from 11 October 2002 to 18 January 2003.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Swiss Federal Council presents final bill for registered partnerships
description: The Federal Council submits its definitive draft of the registered-partnership act to parliament, a procedural step toward the national law for same-sex couples.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Pilot issue of the magazine gay.ch appears
description: The magazine gay.ch publishes its issue zero, covering lifestyle, family, parties and people. From 2003 it appears four times a year, from 2005 six times.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: UBS employees start the pride@UBS network
description: Gay, lesbian and bisexual employees of the bank UBS set up an informal network under the name pride@UBS, later known as UBS Pride Switzerland.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Goliath's Sauna and Texas Lounge raided in Calgary
description: Police raided Goliath's Sauna and the Texas Lounge in Calgary, one of the later bathhouse raids in Canadian history.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Adam Ebbin elected in Virginia
description: Adam Ebbin was elected to the Virginia House of Delegates, becoming the first openly gay member of the Virginia General Assembly.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Armenia decriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Armenia's new criminal code came into force without the Soviet-era article criminalizing sexual relations between men, decriminalizing consensual same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Australia issues first documents showing indeterminate gender
description: Alex MacFarlane became the first person reported to obtain a birth certificate and passport showing indeterminate gender, issued in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria grants limited recognition to same-sex cohabitation
description: Limited legal recognition of same-sex cohabitation came into effect in Austria, without joint adoption; it was superseded by registered partnerships in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Basel youth group Rose disbands
description: The gay youth group Rose in Basel dissolved toward the end of the year. The youth group grenzenlos took over as its successor.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Belize recriminalizes same-sex relations
description: Belize was reported to have recriminalized consensual same-sex sexual activity; Section 53 of its criminal code continued to penalize such acts until it was struck down in 2016.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: British Columbia and Ontario begin marrying same-sex couples
description: Court rulings clear the way for British Columbia and Ontario to begin issuing marriage licences to same-sex couples, ahead of nationwide recognition.

sources.label: UFCW Canada — LGBTQ History Timeline
Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Buenos Aires civil union law takes effect
description: The civil union law of the city of Buenos Aires came into force, allowing same-sex couples to register the first legally recognized unions in Latin America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer airs pioneering lesbian bedroom scene
description: Buffy the Vampire Slayer showed girlfriends Willow Rosenberg and Tara Maclay in bed together, regarded as the first scene of its kind on a US broadcast network series.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bulgaria bans discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: Bulgaria adopted anti-discrimination legislation covering sexual orientation across all sectors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Central Switzerland conference launches its magazine UPDATE
description: UPDATE, the magazine and mouthpiece of the LesBiSchwule Konferenz of central Switzerland, appears for the first time, with five issues planned per year.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Chuck Wolfe becomes CEO of the Victory Fund
description: Chuck Wolfe became chief executive of the Victory Fund and Victory Institute, leading the organizations' national campaign and leadership programs.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Club Isola in Basel closes
description: The gay club Isola at Gempenstrasse 60 in Basel shut its doors, ending its run as a meeting place for the local scene.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Conservative Judaism recognizes sex reassignment in Jewish law
description: The Committee on Jewish Law and Standards approved a ruling that sex reassignment surgery is a permissible treatment for gender dysphoria and that it changes a transgender person's sex status under Jewish law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Croatia enacts law on same-sex unions
description: Croatia's law on same-sex civil unions came into effect, granting limited rights to cohabiting same-sex couples without registration or adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Employment Equality (Sexual Orientation) Regulations 2003
description: The regulations put comprehensive workplace protection in place for all sexual orientations for the first time in England, Wales and Scotland.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024
National Museums Liverpool — LGBTQ+ timeline
Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality
Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Final conference held at Boldern
description: The last in the long-running series of conferences for homosexual people at the Boldern center above Lake Zurich takes place, closing a decades-old meeting tradition.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: First lesbian sex scene airs on US broadcast television
description: An episode of Buffy the Vampire Slayer included what is considered the first lesbian sex scene in US broadcast television history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five US states expand anti-discrimination protections
description: Arizona, Kentucky, Michigan, New Mexico and Pennsylvania added protections covering sexual orientation and in some cases gender identity, in scopes ranging from public-sector employment to full private-sector coverage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gene Robinson becomes first openly gay Episcopal bishop
description: Gene Robinson was consecrated bishop of New Hampshire, becoming the first openly gay bishop in the Episcopal Church in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Group annersoberwallis founded in Brig
description: In Brig, canton of Valais, the group annersoberwallis is established for gay men and lesbians in the Upper Valais region.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Homosexuality decriminalized in Iraq
description: With the restoration of Iraq's 1969 penal code after the 2003 invasion, consensual same-sex relations were no longer criminalized under national law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jennifer Finney Boylan's memoir becomes a bestseller
description: She's Not There: A Life in Two Genders by Jennifer Finney Boylan became the first bestselling book by an openly transgender American author.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kitzinger and Wilkinson marry abroad, unrecognised at home
description: Celia Kitzinger and Sue Wilkinson legally married in Canada, but UK law refused to recognise the marriage, prompting a legal challenge.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Les Homos group in Schaffhausen disbands
description: The group Les Homos in Schaffhausen dissolves, ending its activity in the canton.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: New South Wales and Northern Territory equalize age of consent
description: The Australian state of New South Wales and the Northern Territory set equal ages of consent for same-sex and different-sex sexual activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patrick Harvie becomes first openly bisexual MSP
description: Patrick Harvie was elected to the Scottish Parliament, becoming its first openly bisexual member.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queer gardeners organize as Querbeet
description: Gay, lesbian, bi and transgender gardeners form the group Querbeet, which belongs to the workplace section of Pink Cross.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Reuben Zellman admitted to Reform rabbinical school
description: Reuben Zellman became the first openly transgender person accepted to Hebrew Union College-Jewish Institute of Religion; he was ordained in 2010.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ron Oden elected first Black openly gay US mayor
description: Ron Oden was elected mayor of Palm Springs, California, becoming the first openly gay Black mayor in the United States. The source's stated location of Kansas appears to be an error.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Russia ends ban on gay people in the military
description: Russia's formal ban on gay people serving in the armed forces ended, although restrictions tied to medical and psychiatric assessments persisted in practice.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stonewall Cymru launches in Wales
description: Stonewall Cymru was established to campaign for LGBTQ+ equality specifically in Wales.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Tasmania passes relationship registration law
description: The Australian state of Tasmania passed legislation creating registered partnerships for same-sex couples, with step-child adoption only.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tödigay group forms in the canton of Glarus
description: Homosexual people from across the canton of Glarus come together in a group named Tödigay, after the canton's highest mountain.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Trans and intersex seminar held at Reconstructionist Rabbinical College
description: Rabbi Margaret Wenig organized the first school-wide seminar at the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College addressing the psychological, legal, and religious issues affecting intersex and transsexual people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK government states transsexualism is not a mental illness
description: The Lord Chancellor's Office published Government Policy Concerning Transsexual People, stating categorically that transsexualism is not a mental illness.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: United Kingdom abolishes buggery as a legal concept
description: UK sexual offences reform removed buggery as a distinct criminal offence, eliminating the last statutory remnant of the historic criminalization of same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Youth group Caméléon founded in Winterthur
description: A youth group named Caméléon is set up in Winterthur for young queer people in the region.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: United Kingdom legalizes adoption by same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to jointly adopt children in United Kingdom.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: European Court of Human Rights ranks anti-gay discrimination with racial discrimination
description: The European Court of Human Rights holds that discrimination against homosexual and bisexual people is as serious as discrimination based on race, origin, skin color or sex.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Traveling exhibition derBUS begins its tour in Dagmersellen
description: The traveling exhibition derBUS is shown for the first time in Dagmersellen, canton of Lucerne, presenting the lives of lesbians and gay men to mainly rural audiences. Its tour through 15 cantons lasts over five years, closing in Zurich on 18 May 2008.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: New South Wales equalises the age of consent
description: Attorney-General Bob Debus introduced a bill that lowered the male homosexual age of consent to 16, matching the existing heterosexual age of consent.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: RADIGAL forms as LGBTI section of the Swiss liberal party
description: RADIGAL is established as the LGBTI specialist group of the Swiss liberal party FDP and its youth wing, anchoring queer advocacy inside the party.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Canton of Zurich opens partnership registration to binational couples
description: From this day, binational same-sex couples in the canton of Zurich may register their partnership even while the foreign partner's residence status remains unresolved.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Gay and lesbian choir festival fills two Zurich concert halls
description: The SchwuLesbisches Chorspektakel, a festival of gay and lesbian choirs, takes place at the Volkshaus and the Tonhalle in Zurich over four days.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: First legal same-sex marriage in Canada in Toronto
description: Michael Leshner and Michael Stark married in Toronto immediately after the Ontario court ruling, becoming the first same-sex couple to legally marry in Canada.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Zurich CSD marches under the motto landauf - stadtab
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day takes place with the motto "landauf - stadtab", linking city and countryside in its message.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Zurich partnership law takes effect with first couple registered
description: The cantonal act on same-sex partnerships enters into force in Zurich. A first couple is officially registered at the Stadthaus, the earliest legal registration of a same-sex partnership in Switzerland.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride held in Delémont
description: Delémont, capital of the canton of Jura, hosts a Lesbian & Gay Pride, bringing the march to the young canton.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: First tri-national CSD 3Land held in Basel
description: Basel stages the first CSD "3Land" for the Regio Basiliensis, the border region spanning Switzerland, Germany and France.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: HAZ sets up working group on schools
description: The Zurich organization HAZ forms a working group on schools, with subgroups for gay and lesbian teachers in the canton, education policy, and classroom visits.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Petition for a Vaud partnership law handed in at Lausanne
description: The organizations VoGay and Lilith submit a petition with 3,000 signatures in Lausanne, demanding a cantonal partnership law for same-sex couples.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Gay and lesbian groups meet for a hike on the Jakob Stutz trail
description: For a day of encounters between gay and lesbian groups, participants take a guided hike along the Jakob Stutz trail from Pfäffikon to Wila in the Tösstal, canton of Zurich.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: National campaign body Ja zur Partnerschaft founded
description: Pink Cross, network, LOS and FELS establish the national association "Ja zur Partnerschaft" to campaign for the Swiss registered-partnership act.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: First official church blessing of a same-sex couple in Zurich
description: On Coming Out Day, the first official church wedding ceremony for a same-sex couple takes place in the Reformed Predigerkirche in Zurich. The same day, the parish board of Winterthur-Seen refuses such a ceremony for the canton's second registered couple.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: GayRadio starts broadcasting on Radio RaBe in Bern
description: A fortnightly program named GayRadio, today's QueerUp Radio, goes on air on the community station Radio RaBe in Bern. It can later also be heard on Radio LoRa in Zurich.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Groups hold first coordination meeting on youth and schools
description: Swiss groups working on the topic of youth and schools come together for their first coordination meeting, pooling efforts on education outreach.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Swiss National Council approves registered-partnership bill
description: The National Council adopts the federal act on registered partnerships for same-sex couples by 118 votes to 50, moving the bill forward in the parliamentary process that leads to the 2005 national referendum.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab-2000

title: Australia writes a same-sex marriage ban into federal law
description: Australia's federal parliament amended the Marriage Act in 2004 to define marriage as a union of a man and a woman, ruling out recognition of same-sex marriages.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Awareness group La Boussole forms in Romandy
description: La Boussole, a group raising awareness of sexual orientation and diverse identities, formed in French-speaking Switzerland, affiliated with Pink Cross and the lesbian organization LOS.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Basel-Stadt anchors same-sex partnerships in pension law
description: The canton of Basel-Stadt wrote recognition of same-sex partnerships into its new pension-fund law.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Bern city pension fund to treat same-sex couples equally
description: Following a 54-16 vote in the Bern city parliament, the municipal pension fund resolved to grant same-sex couples equal treatment starting in 2006.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Bern high court extends victim-support law to same-sex partners
description: The high court of the canton of Bern ruled that the victim-assistance law also applies to partners in same-sex couples.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Bhutan criminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: Bhutan enacted a legal prohibition on consensual same-sex sexual activity between adults, making homosexuality a punishable offense.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Bisi Alimi comes out on Nigerian television
description: Activist Bisi Alimi spoke openly about being gay on Nigerian television in 2004, the first person in the country to do so on air.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cape Verde decriminalizes same-sex activity
description: A new penal code took effect in Cape Verde in 2004 without provisions against consensual same-sex relations, ending their criminalization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Cape Verde equalizes the age of consent
description: Cape Verde set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Civil Partnership Act 2004 introduces same-sex partnerships
description: The Civil Partnership Act 2004 introduced legal recognition for same-sex couples. Civil partnerships became available from 2005 in England and Wales, with Scotland and Northern Ireland following.

title: Final issue of spot news appears
description: The newsletter spot news, published by the Zurich youth group spot 25, released its last issue around the start of 2004.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: First all-transgender performance of The Vagina Monologues
description: Eighteen transgender women performed The Vagina Monologues in 2004 in the production's first all-transgender staging, which added a new monologue about trans women's experiences.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Five US states adopt constitutional same-sex marriage bans
description: Voters in Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, Oregon and Utah approved state constitutional amendments in 2004 barring same-sex couples from marrying.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gay Film Rapperswil-Jona emerges from Klick
description: The group Klick in Rapperswil-Jona reorganized itself under the name Gay Film Rapperswil-Jona.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Gay Fricktal group forms
description: Homosexual women and men from the Fricktal region of Aargau, the Basel countryside and adjacent areas came together as the group Gay Fricktal.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel ab 2000

title: Gender Recognition Act 2004 enables legal transition
description: The Gender Recognition Act enabled adults in the UK to transition legally and be issued new birth certificates, with protection for their previous identity. The process was heavily medicalised.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

Shay & Blue — LGBT History Month timeline
Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Germany allows step-adoption for registered partners
description: Germany extended step-adoption to registered same-sex partners in 2004, letting a partner legally adopt the other partner's biological child.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Indiana, Louisiana and Maine expand discrimination protections
description: In 2004, Indiana added gender identity and Louisiana added sexual orientation to public-sector employment protections, while Maine adopted broader anti-discrimination coverage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Karamo Brown becomes the first out gay Black man on reality TV
description: Karamo Brown joined the cast of MTV's The Real World: Philadelphia in 2004, becoming the first openly gay Black man on a US reality series.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luna is the first mainstream young-adult novel with a trans lead
description: Julie Anne Peters' novel Luna appeared in 2004 as the first young-adult book from a major publisher to center a transgender character.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lupe Valdez elected sheriff in Texas
description: Lupe Valdez was elected sheriff of Dallas County, Texas, becoming the first openly lesbian Latina sheriff in the United States.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Maine enacts domestic partnership law
description: The US state of Maine passed and brought into effect a domestic partnership law providing limited legal recognition for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey enacts a domestic partnership law
description: The US state of New Jersey passed a limited domestic partnership law in 2004, and it took effect the same year, giving same-sex couples a restricted set of legal protections.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey governor James McGreevey comes out as gay
description: James McGreevey came out in 2004 while serving as governor of New Jersey, the first sitting US state governor to do so openly. He resigned from office shortly afterward.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Zealand passes the Civil Union Act
description: New Zealand's parliament approved civil unions for same-sex couples in 2004. The law offered legal recognition of partnerships but did not include joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Portugal adds sexual orientation to its discrimination protections
description: Portugal adopted anti-discrimination protections covering sexual orientation in 2004, anchored by a constitutional revision that added the ground to its equality clause.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queer Kinok film screenings return to St. Gallen
description: St. Gallen saw queer film screenings for the second time after 1999, now presented under the name Queer Kinok.

California courts later voided the 2004 marriages; the couple wed again in 2008 when the state supreme court opened marriage to same-sex couples.

title: Norway passes gender-neutral marriage law

title: Angie Zapata murdered in Colorado
description: Transgender woman Angie Zapata is killed in Greeley, Colorado. Her killer, Allen Andrade, is later convicted of first-degree murder and a bias-motivated crime — the first US hate-crime conviction for the killing of a transgender victim.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Panama equalizes the age of consent
description: Panama set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

title: Matthew Mitcham wins Olympic gold as an openly gay athlete
description: Australian diver Matthew Mitcham takes gold at the Beijing Games, the first openly gay man to win an Olympic gold medal.

title: Rachel Maddow debuts as prime-time news anchor
description: MSNBC launches The Rachel Maddow Show, making Maddow the first openly gay or lesbian anchor of a major US prime-time news program.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Basel gets its first gay film festival, Luststreifen
description: Luststreifen, the first gay film festival in Basel, runs from 11 to 14 September 2008.

title: Arizona and California voters approve same-sex marriage bans
description: Ballot measures in Arizona (Proposition 102) and California (Proposition 8) amend the state constitutions to restrict marriage to different-sex couples, ending the marriages that had begun in California earlier that year.

title: Arkansas bars unmarried couples from adopting and fostering
description: Arkansas voters approve Act 1, blocking unmarried cohabiting couples — including same-sex couples, who could not marry — from adopting or fostering children. The state supreme court struck the measure down in 2011.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Florida bans same-sex marriage and civil unions
description: Florida voters approve Amendment 2, a constitutional amendment barring both same-sex marriage and any legal union treated as equivalent to marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kate Brown becomes first openly bisexual statewide officeholder in the US
description: Oregon voters elect Kate Brown as secretary of state, making her the first openly bisexual person to hold statewide office in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stu Rasmussen elected as America's first openly transgender mayor
description: Voters in Silverton, Oregon elect Stu Rasmussen as mayor, the first openly transgender person elected to a mayoralty in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California voters pass Proposition 8
description: California voters approved Proposition 8, banning same-sex marriage; a federal judge later found the measure unconstitutional.

description: Jerry Buttimer, John Lyons and Dominic Hannigan took their seats as the first openly LGBT members of the Dail Eireann, representing Cork South-Central, Dublin North-West and Meath East. Katherine Zappone became the first openly lesbian senator.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Fred Karger launches first openly gay Republican presidential bid
description: Fred Karger entered the race for the 2012 Republican presidential nomination, the first openly gay Republican to seek the US presidency. His campaign was unsuccessful.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harmony Santana earns a major acting award nomination
description: Harmony Santana became the first openly transgender actress to receive a major acting award nomination, named by the Independent Spirit Awards in 2011 as Best Supporting Actress for Gun Hill Road.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Illinois and Rhode Island enact civil union laws
description: In 2011 the US states of Illinois (with joint adoption rights) and Rhode Island passed civil union laws that took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Isle of Man enacts civil partnerships
description: The Isle of Man passed and brought into force a civil partnership law in 2011, including joint adoption rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Isle of Man introduces civil partnerships and adoption
description: The Civil Partnership Act 2011 introduced civil partnerships on the Isle of Man and also provided for adoption by same-sex couples.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Jaiyah Saelua plays World Cup qualifier as first openly trans footballer
description: Taking the field for American Samoa in Oceania qualifying for the 2014 World Cup, Jaiyah Saelua became the first openly transgender player in international football.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: LaWana Mayfield elected to Charlotte City Council
description: LaWana Mayfield won a seat on the Charlotte City Council in North Carolina, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of the body.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Lifetime blood donation ban on MSM is lifted
description: In 2011 the United Kingdom replaced the lifetime blood donation ban on men who have sex with men with a one-year deferral period. Northern Ireland retained the lifetime ban until 2016.

title: Malawi criminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: Malawi enacted a legal prohibition on consensual same-sex sexual activity between adults, making homosexuality a punishable offense.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Peter Tatchell Foundation established
description: The Peter Tatchell Foundation was founded to work on human rights and to assist LGBT+ migrants to the UK.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) opens ordination to gay and lesbian ministers
description: The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.) voted to permit the ordination of openly gay and lesbian clergy, ending its previous exclusion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rachel Isaacs ordained as first openly lesbian Conservative rabbi
description: The Jewish Theological Seminary ordained Rachel Isaacs, the first openly lesbian rabbi in Judaism's Conservative movement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Repeal of Don't Ask, Don't Tell
description: The United States repealed the Don't Ask, Don't Tell military policy, allowing gay, lesbian, and bisexual service members to serve openly in the armed forces.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: Robin Kniech elected to Denver City Council
description: Robin Kniech won an at-large seat on the Denver City Council, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of the council.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Ruth Davidson becomes first out party leader
description: Ruth Davidson was elected leader of the Scottish Conservatives, becoming the first openly gay leader of a UK political party.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Same-sex couple shares ceremonial first kiss at US Navy homecoming
description: Petty Officers Marissa Gaeta and Citlalic Snell were selected for the traditional first kiss when their ship returned to port, the first same-sex couple given that honor in the US Navy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco publishes first governmental report on bisexual invisibility
description: San Francisco's Human Rights Commission issued "Bisexual Invisibility: Impacts and Regulations", the first report on bisexual visibility released by any government body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sandra Lawson admitted to Reconstructionist Rabbinical College
description: Sandra Lawson became the first African-American — and the first openly gay African-American — admitted to the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Suquamish Tribe legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Suquamish Tribe adopted marriage equality in 2011, with the change taking effect that year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United States ends the ban on openly gay, lesbian and bisexual military service
description: With the 2011 repeal of the Don't Ask, Don't Tell policy, openly gay, lesbian and bisexual people gained the right to serve in the US armed forces.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Institute of Medicine reports on LGBT health disparities
description: The US Institute of Medicine published Health of Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender People, a landmark report assessing health disparities among LGBT people and setting out a research agenda for the National Institutes of Health.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: Saskatchewan bars marriage officers from refusing couples
description: Saskatchewan courts ruled that marriage commissioners cannot refuse to marry same-sex couples on religious grounds.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Obama administration stops defending DOMA
description: On 23 February 2011 President Barack Obama announced that his administration would no longer defend the Defense of Marriage Act in court, viewing its core provision as unconstitutional.

sources.label: PBS American Experience — Milestones in the American Gay Rights Movement

title: UN Human Rights Council passes its first LGBT rights resolution
description: A South African resolution requesting a study on discrimination based on sexual orientation passed the UN Human Rights Council 23-19, the first time any UN body approved a resolution affirming LGBT rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zurich Pride Festival 2011 embraces difference
description: The Zurich Pride Festival runs from 17 to 19 June 2011 under the bilingual motto "Live with the difference – vivre avec la différence".

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010–2019

title: National Council committee rejects the Familienchancen petition
description: The legal-affairs committee of the Swiss National Council votes 13 to 10 against the petition "Familienchancen" on 23 June 2011.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010–2019

title: New York legalizes same-sex marriage
description: On 24 June 2011 New York passed the Marriage Equality Act, becoming the largest U.S. state up to that point to legalize same-sex marriage.

sources.label: PBS American Experience — Milestones in the American Gay Rights Movement

Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Council of States committee backs adoption rights for registered couples
description: On 20 October 2011 the legal-affairs committee of the Swiss Council of States endorses the petition "Familienchancen", filing a motion titled "Gleiche Chancen für alle Familien" that asks the government to allow adoption within the partnership law.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010–2019

title: Tony Briffa is elected mayor of Hobsons Bay
description: Tony Briffa's election in November 2011 in the City of Hobsons Bay, in the suburbs of Melbourne, Australia, is believed to have made them the world's first intersex mayor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Book about the couple Röbi Rapp as well as Ernst Ostertag launches
description: Barbara Bosshard's book "Verborgene Liebe", telling the life story of the couple Röbi Rapp plus Ernst Ostertag, premieres at its launch event on 3 November 2011.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010–2019

title: Elio Di Rupo becomes Prime Minister of Belgium
description: Elio Di Rupo took office on 6 December 2011 as the first openly gay man to head a national government.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Berkeley proclaims first US city day recognizing bisexual people
description: Berkeley, California, became the first US city to officially proclaim a day recognizing bisexual people, designating 23 September as Bisexual Pride and Bi Visibility Day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Brian Sims elected as Pennsylvania's first out gay candidate for the legislature
description: Brian Sims became the first Pennsylvania state legislator elected while openly gay. Before Sims took office, sitting Rep. Mike Fleck came out, making Fleck the state's first openly gay serving legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California enacts first US state ban on conversion therapy
description: California became the first US state to sign into law a ban on therapy claiming to change gay people's sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile enacts anti-discrimination law covering orientation and gender identity
description: Chile adopted national anti-discrimination legislation that includes sexual orientation and gender identity as protected grounds.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Richardson elected as Florida's first openly gay state legislator
description: David Richardson won election to the Florida legislature, the state's first openly gay state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Emily Aviva Kapor becomes first openly transgender female rabbi
description: Emily Aviva Kapor, an American rabbi ordained privately in 2005, began living as a woman, making her the first openly transgender female rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: FDA approves Truvada for HIV prevention
description: The US Food and Drug Administration approved Truvada as the first drug to prevent HIV acquisition, taken daily by those at risk. It marked the start of pre-exposure prophylaxis as an HIV prevention tool.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

title: First same-sex engagement takes place at the White House
description: Ben Schock and Matthew Phelps became the first same-sex couple to get engaged inside the White House.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex weddings at West Point and on a US military base
description: Ellen Schick and Shannon Simpson held the first same-sex marriage at the US Military Academy's Old Cadet Chapel, while Elny and Anacelly McKinney became the first known same-sex couple to marry on a US military base, at Naval Base Point Loma in San Diego.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hawaii civil-union law takes effect
description: Civil unions for same-sex couples became available in the US state of Hawaii as its civil-union law came into force.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Historic convictions can be removed under new law
description: The Protection of Freedoms Act allowed men to apply to have historic convictions for consensual same-sex acts, no longer offences, removed from their records.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Israeli family court grants the country's first same-sex divorce
description: The Ramat Gan Family Court granted a divorce to Avi Even, Israel's first openly gay Knesset member, and Amit Kama, and ordered the Interior Ministry to register them as divorced — the first same-sex divorce in Israel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jacob Candelaria elected as New Mexico's first openly gay male legislator
description: Jacob Candelaria won election to the New Mexico legislature as the state's first openly gay male state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Josh Boschee elected as North Dakota's first openly gay legislator
description: Josh Boschee won election to the North Dakota legislature, the state's first openly gay lawmaker.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kate McKinnon joins Saturday Night Live as first openly lesbian cast member
description: Kate McKinnon became the first openly lesbian cast member of Saturday Night Live. Earlier cast member Danitra Vance was only revealed to be a lesbian after her death.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katie Ricks ordained as first open lesbian in the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.)
description: Katie Ricks became the first open lesbian ordained by the Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.), following the denomination's 2011 vote to allow gay and lesbian clergy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kyrsten Sinema elected as first openly bisexual member of the US Congress
description: Arizona Democrat Kyrsten Sinema won election to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly bisexual person elected to the US Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lesotho decriminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: Lesotho repealed the laws that criminalized consensual same-sex sexual activity between adults, ending the legal prohibition on homosexuality.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline
Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maine, Washington and the Port Gamble S'Klallam Tribe adopt same-sex marriage
description: Same-sex marriage was approved and took effect in the US states of Maine and Washington — both by popular vote — and in the Port Gamble S'Klallam Tribe's jurisdiction.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Manitoba and Ontario add gender identity to human-rights protections
description: The Canadian province of Manitoba added gender identity to its anti-discrimination protections, and Ontario added gender identity and gender expression to its Human Rights Code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Ferrandino chosen as Colorado's first openly gay House speaker
description: Colorado Democrats selected Mark Ferrandino as speaker of the state House, the first openly gay person to hold that office in the state's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Pocan elected to succeed Tammy Baldwin in the US House
description: Mark Pocan won Wisconsin's 2nd Congressional District, the first openly gay candidate to succeed an openly gay member of the US Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mark Takano elected as first openly gay person of color in the US House
description: California's 41st Congressional District elected Mark Takano, the first openly gay person of color to win a seat in the US House of Representatives.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marlene Pray becomes Pennsylvania's first openly bisexual officeholder
description: Marlene Pray joined the borough council of Doylestown, Pennsylvania, as the state's first openly bisexual holder of public office. She resigned the seat in 2013.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maryland passes same-sex marriage law
description: The US state of Maryland passed legislation allowing same-sex marriage; the law was later upheld by referendum before taking effect.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Northern Ireland court legalises same-sex couple adoption
description: Adoption by unmarried and same-sex couples was legalised after rulings in Northern Ireland, confirmed by the Court of Appeal in 2013.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

description: The Ofsted school inspection framework was updated to include explicit reference to homophobic bullying, prompting schools to address it.

title: Openly gay candidate runs for US president
description: An openly gay Republican mounted a campaign for the US presidency, one of the earliest openly gay major-party presidential bids.

description: Puerto Rican featherweight Orlando Cruz publicly came out as gay, the first professional boxer in the world to do so while still competing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ParaNorman features first openly gay character in mainstream animation
description: The animated film ParaNorman included the first openly gay character to appear in a mainstream animated feature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rainbow Jews oral-history project launches in the United Kingdom
description: Rainbow Jews, an oral-history project documenting the lives of Jewish LGBT people in Britain since the 1950s, was launched as the UK's first archive of Jewish LGBT history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Richard Grenell serves as first openly gay Republican campaign spokesman

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage begins in the Mexican state of Quintana Roo
description: Same-sex marriage was adopted and took effect in Quintana Roo, extending marriage equality to another Mexican state after Mexico City.

title: Same-sex partner pins colonel wings on Air Force officer Ginger Wallace
description: Kathy Knopf pinned colonel wings on her partner, Air Force Col. Ginger Wallace — the first known time an out US service member's same-sex partner took part in the pinning tradition previously reserved for spouses and family.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stacie Laughton wins first US state-legislature seat for an openly trans person
description: Stacie Laughton won a seat in the New Hampshire House of Representatives, the first openly transgender person elected to any US state legislature. She resigned before taking office after a prior fraud conviction became public.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stephen Skinner elected as West Virginia's first openly gay legislator
description: Stephen Skinner won a seat in the West Virginia legislature, becoming the state's first openly gay state legislator.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stonewall publishes a major survey of gay and bisexual men's health
description: Stonewall published a survey of gay and bisexual men's health needs with 6,861 respondents across Britain. It found that many needs went unmet, with particular concern over mental health and drug use.

sources.label: Royal Society of Medicine — Snapshots in LGBTQ+ Medical History

description: Fish Huang and You Ya-ting were married in Taiwan's first same-sex Buddhist wedding ceremony, with Buddhist master Shih Chao-hui presiding.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tammy Smith becomes first openly gay active-duty US general
description: Army Reserve officer Tammy Smith was promoted to brigadier general at the Women's Memorial at Arlington National Cemetery, the first openly gay general on active duty in US military history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Bisexuality Report published in the United Kingdom
description: Researchers led by Meg Barker with colleagues from the Open University and BiUK issued The Bisexuality Report, the first report of its kind in the UK, summarizing evidence and recommending steps for bisexual inclusion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US housing department bars LGBT discrimination in assisted housing
description: The US Department of Housing and Urban Development issued a regulation prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity in federally assisted housing programs.

title: Andy Herren wins US Big Brother as first openly gay champion
description: Andy Herren won the American edition of the reality show Big Brother, its first openly gay winner.

description: In 2013 a six-year-old girl named Luana became the first transgender child in Argentina to have her name officially changed on her identity documents, believed to be the youngest person to use the country's 2012 Gender Identity Law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australian Capital Territory marriage law passed, then struck down

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

description: Neuroscientist Ben Barres was elected to the US National Academy of Sciences in 2013, its first openly transgender member.

title: Benjamin Medrano elected as Mexico's first openly gay mayor
description: Benjamin Medrano won the mayoralty of Fresnillo in 2013, the first openly gay person elected mayor in Mexico.

title: BiLaw, first US national association of bisexual lawyers, forms
description: BiLaw was established as the first American national organization bringing together bisexual lawyers, law professors, law students and allies.

title: Bradford City becomes first UK club to fly the rainbow flag
description: Bradford City, known as the Bantams, became the first UK league football club to fly the rainbow flag at the start of the 2013/14 season, following anti-racist and anti-homophobic work with Unite activist Ann Morgan.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: California enacts first US law protecting transgender students
description: California's School Success and Opportunity Act, enacted in 2013, was the first US law protecting transgender students, guaranteeing public-school pupils participation in sex-segregated programs and access to facilities matching their gender identity.

title: California recommends a transgender-themed book for schools
description: In 2013 the California Department of Education's recommended reading list for grades pre-K through 12 included a book with a transgender theme for the first time, I Am J by Cris Beam.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Campeche enacts civil unions
description: The Mexican state of Campeche passed a civil union law in 2013 and it came into force the same year, giving same-sex couples legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cason Crane completes the Seven Summits
description: In 2013 climber Cason Crane became the first openly gay man to summit the Seven Summits and the first person to carry the rainbow flag to the top of Mount Everest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
