description: Arizona State backup offensive lineman Edward Sarafin came out as gay while on the roster, the first active NCAA Division I football player to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland issues postage stamps honoring Tom of Finland
description: Finland's postal service released stamps celebrating Touko Laaksonen, the homoerotic artist known as Tom of Finland, bringing his work onto official state stamps.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to marry in Finland, granting them the same marriage rights as opposite-sex couples.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: First bodybuilding competition for transgender men held
description: The FTM Fitness Conference staged the FTM Fitness World Bodybuilding Competition, the first bodybuilding contest exclusively for transgender men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Jersey Pride held
description: The first Jersey Pride event took place.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: First openly transgender woman marries in Malta
description: Malta saw its first wedding of an openly transgender woman, a personal milestone that followed the country's advances in gender recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex marriage entered into public record in Mississippi
description: Anna Guillot and Chrissy Kelly, married in New York in 2012, became the first same-sex couple to place their marriage on public record in Mississippi, though the state did not recognize it legally.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First serving Chilean soldier comes out as gay
description: Marine Mauricio Ruiz announced he is gay, the first active member of Chile's armed forces to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US national campaign ad to feature a same-sex couple airs
description: Senate candidate Monica Wehby ran a campaign advertisement showing couple Ben West and Paul Rummell, the first ad for American national office to feature a same-sex couple.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gibraltar introduces civil partnerships with joint adoption
description: Gibraltar, a British overseas territory, passed and brought into force a civil partnership law that includes joint adoption rights for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hong Kong hosts its first international LGBTI rights symposium
description: Hong Kong convened its first international symposium devoted to the rights of LGBTI people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ICEIS Rain performs at Aboriginal Peoples Choice Music Awards as first two-spirit artist
description: Singer ICEIS Rain became the first openly two-spirit performer to appear on stage at Canada's Aboriginal Peoples Choice Music Awards.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italian court approves first adoption within a same-sex couple
description: An Italian court for the first time allowed the partner of a child's biological mother to adopt the girl, letting both women in the couple become legal parents.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italy legally recognizes a foreign same-sex marriage for the first time
description: The marriage of Giuseppe Chigiotti and Stefano Bucci, performed in New York in 2012, became the first overseas same-sex marriage to receive legal recognition in Italy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jim Ferlo becomes the Pennsylvania Senate's first openly gay member
description: State Senator Jim Ferlo came out publicly, making him the first openly gay legislator to serve in the Pennsylvania Senate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Judith Ellen Levy confirmed as Michigan's first openly lesbian federal judge
description: The US Senate confirmed Judith Ellen Levy to the federal bench, making her the first openly lesbian federal judge in Michigan.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kinnon MacKinnon wins Gay Games powerlifting gold as openly transgender man
description: At the 2014 Gay Games in Cleveland, Kinnon MacKinnon took a gold medal in powerlifting, the first openly transgender man to do so at the Games.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lauren Lubin runs New York City Marathon as first openly non-binary athlete
description: Lauren Lubin competed in the New York City Marathon as the race's first openly non-binary athlete.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox lands Time cover and a first transgender Emmy nomination
description: Laverne Cox appeared on the cover of Time's 9 June 2014 issue for "The Transgender Tipping Point" and became the first openly transgender person nominated for an acting Emmy, for her role in Orange Is the New Black.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lea T fronts Redken as first openly transgender face of a global cosmetics brand
description: Brazilian model Lea T signed as the face of the American hair-care brand Redken, the first time an openly transgender model represented a cosmetics brand worldwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Liberate formed to represent Channel Islands LGBT+ community
description: Liberate, a group representing the LGBT+ community and minorities in the Channel Islands, was established in Guernsey in February and in Jersey in August.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Los Tigres del Norte release the first norteño song about lesbian love
description: The album Realidades by Los Tigres del Norte included "Era Diferente", a song about a lesbian teenager in love with her best friend — described by bandleader Jorge Hernandez as the first gay love song by a norteño group.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luxembourg passes same-sex marriage law
description: Luxembourg's parliament approved legislation opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maite Oronoz Rodriguez confirmed to Puerto Rico's Supreme Court
description: Maite Oronoz Rodriguez was nominated and then confirmed to Puerto Rico's Supreme Court, the first openly gay person to reach the territory's highest bench.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malta legalizes adoption by same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to jointly adopt children in Malta.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Malta writes gender identity into its constitution as a protected category
description: Malta added gender identity to the protected categories in its constitution, the first European state to give it constitutional protection.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maria Walsh becomes first openly gay Rose of Tralee
description: After winning Ireland's Rose of Tralee title, Maria Walsh came out as gay, the first openly gay holder of the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Matthew Muir sworn in as first openly gay judge on New Zealand's High Court
description: Matthew Muir joined the bench of New Zealand's High Court, the first openly gay judge to sit on it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maura Healey elected first openly gay state attorney general in the US
description: Voters in Massachusetts elected Maura Healey as attorney general, the first openly gay person to win a state attorney general's office anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Meghan Stabler named Working Mother of the Year
description: Meghan Stabler became the first openly transgender woman to receive Working Mother magazine's Working Mother of the Year award.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mikie Goldstein installed as Israel's first openly gay Conservative pulpit rabbi
description: Congregation Adat Shalom-Emanuel in Rehovot installed Mikie Goldstein as its spiritual leader, the Israeli Conservative movement's first openly gay congregational rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mikie Goldstein ordained as first openly gay Conservative Jewish rabbi
description: Mikie Goldstein received ordination in Judaism's Conservative movement, its first openly gay man to be ordained as a rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mills College and Mount Holyoke open admission to transgender students
description: Mills College became the first single-sex US college to adopt a policy explicitly welcoming transgender students, and Mount Holyoke became the first of the Seven Sisters colleges to admit them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Native American tribal nations adopt same-sex marriage
description: In 2014 several sovereign tribal nations in the United States legalized same-sex marriage, including the Puyallup Tribe, the Pascua Yaqui Tribe, the Salt River Pima-Maricopa Indian Community, the San Carlos Apache Tribe, the Blackfoot Tribe and others.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nehirim holds first retreat for LGBT Jewish clergy
description: The Jewish organization Nehirim convened its first retreat for LGBT rabbis, rabbinic pastors, cantors, and students of those professions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Northern Cyprus decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Northern Cyprus repealed its criminal ban on same-sex acts in 2014, the last territory in Europe to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: OutVets marches in Boston's Veterans Day parade
description: A contingent from OutVets took part in Boston's Veterans Day parade, the first LGBT organization ever to march in it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Padmini Prakash becomes India's first openly transgender news anchor
description: Padmini Prakash began presenting television news in India, the country's first openly transgender news anchor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pakistan's first anti-homophobia children's book appears in print
description: My Chacha Is Gay, written and illustrated by the Canada-based artist Eiynah, was released in print as Pakistan's first children's book challenging homophobia, after first circulating online.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Palau decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Palau repealed its law criminalizing consensual same-sex acts in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pascal Tessier becomes first known openly gay Eagle Scout
description: Pascal Tessier, a 17-year-old from Chevy Chase, Maryland, attained the rank of Eagle Scout, the first known openly gay Boy Scout to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Peru gains its first openly transgender official and first openly gay congressman
description: Luisa Revilla Urcia won a council seat in La Esperanza, Trujillo province, the first openly transgender person elected to public office in Peru. Congressman Carlos Bruce later came out, the first openly gay member of Peru's Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Petra De Sutter enters Belgian Senate as first openly transgender parliamentarian
description: Petra De Sutter took a seat in Belgium's Senate, the first openly transgender person to serve in the country's Parliament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queen Elizabeth II sends greetings to the London LGBT switchboard
description: Queen Elizabeth II sent congratulations to the London Lesbian and Gay Switchboard on its 40th anniversary, regarded as the first public message of support for the LGBT community from the British Crown.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robert Biedroń elected as Poland's first openly gay mayor
description: Voters in Słupsk elected Robert Biedroń mayor, the first openly gay person to lead a Polish city.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robert Garcia elected mayor of Long Beach
description: Robert Garcia was elected mayor of Long Beach, California, becoming the city's first openly gay and first Latino mayor.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Same-sex marriage arrives in nineteen more US states
description: During 2014 same-sex marriage became legal in Oregon, Pennsylvania, Utah, Oklahoma, Virginia, Wisconsin, Indiana, Colorado, Nevada, Idaho, West Virginia, North Carolina, Alaska, Arizona, Wyoming, Kansas, South Carolina and Montana, largely through court rulings striking down state bans.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Illinois
description: Illinois's same-sex marriage law came into force in 2014, making marriage available to same-sex couples statewide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Diego Comic-Con hosts its first transgender panel
description: The Transgender Trends panel at San Diego Comic-Con was the first panel on transgender topics held at the convention.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco police academy graduates first openly trans officer
description: Mikayla Connell became the first openly transgender police officer to graduate from the San Francisco Police academy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco street renamed for trans activist Vicki Marlane
description: The 100 block of Turk Street in San Francisco was renamed Vicki Mar Lane in honor of transgender activist and performer Vicki Marlane.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saskatchewan adds gender identity to anti-discrimination law
description: The Canadian province of Saskatchewan amended its human rights legislation in 2014 to prohibit discrimination based on gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa seats Africa's first openly gay cabinet minister and MP
description: Lynne Brown was appointed to South Africa's cabinet, the first openly gay minister in any African government, while Zakhele Mbhele entered parliament as the first openly gay black MP in any African nation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Strongman Rob Kearney comes out as gay
description: Professional strongman Rob Kearney came out, becoming the first openly gay man actively competing at international professional strongman events.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tel Aviv unveils memorial to LGBT victims of Nazi persecution
description: A memorial honoring LGBT people persecuted by the Nazi regime was unveiled in Tel Aviv, Israel's first monument dedicated to non-Jewish victims of the Holocaust.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tennessee birth certificate lists a woman as "father" for the first time
description: Emilia Maria Jesty, daughter of a lesbian couple, became the first child born in Tennessee whose birth certificate listed a woman in the "father" field.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tim Cook comes out as gay
description: Apple chief executive Tim Cook publicly came out as gay, making him the first openly gay CEO of a Fortune 500 company.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tona Brown performs at Carnegie Hall
description: Violinist and vocalist Tona Brown became the first African-American openly transgender woman to perform at Carnegie Hall in New York City.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Toni Atkins becomes California Assembly Speaker
description: Toni Atkins was elevated to Speaker of the California State Assembly, becoming the first openly lesbian person to hold the post.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Toni Atkins elected first openly lesbian speaker of the California Assembly
description: Toni Atkins became the first openly lesbian speaker of the California State Assembly, and in that role served as the state's acting governor for a day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trans Lifeline, first US suicide hotline for trans people, launches
description: Nina Chaubal and Greta Gustava Martela cofounded Trans Lifeline, the first suicide hotline in the United States dedicated to transgender people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Bangladeshis hold the country's first pride march
description: At least 1,000 openly transgender people marched in Bangladesh's first pride parade, one year after the government legally recognized hijras as a third gender.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Studies Quarterly begins publication
description: Transgender Studies Quarterly, the first non-medical academic journal devoted to transgender issues, published its first issue with Susan Stryker and Paisley Currah as coeditors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Church of Christ sponsors the Gay Games as first religious denomination
description: The United Church of Christ backed Gay Games 9 as a silver-level sponsor, the first religious denomination to act as a major sponsor of the Games.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US government files first Title VII suits for transgender workers
description: The US Equal Employment Opportunity Commission filed its first Title VII lawsuits on behalf of transgender employees, acting for Aimee Stephens and Brandi Branson.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US grants survivor benefits to first known same-sex war widow
description: The US Department of Veterans Affairs agreed to pay survivor benefits to Tracy Dice Johnson, whose wife Donna Johnson was killed in a 2012 suicide bombing, the first known same-sex war widow to receive them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Naval Academy Chapel hosts its first same-sex wedding
description: Academy graduate David Bucher and his partner Bruce Moats were married in the US Naval Academy Chapel, the first same-sex wedding held there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Victoria appoints Australia's first minister dedicated to LGBTI equality
description: The incoming Labor government of Victoria, Australia named Martin Foley Minister for Equality, the first Australian government post dedicated to gay, lesbian, bisexual, transgender and intersex issues.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Wisconsin gets its first openly transgender elected official
description: Gypsy Vered Meltzer won a seat on the Appleton City Council, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to public office in Wisconsin.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: World's first cemetery for lesbians opens in Berlin
description: A burial ground dedicated to lesbians, described as the first of its kind worldwide, was unveiled in Berlin.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sochi Winter Olympics draw worldwide protests over Russia's anti-LGBT laws
description: The 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi prompted protests around the world against host country Russia's crackdown on LGBT rights. Gus Kenworthy, later openly gay, won silver in men's freestyle skiing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Swiss film Der Kreis premieres at the Berlinale
description: Stefan Haupt's docudrama Der Kreis, portraying the Zurich circle around the mid-century gay journal of the same name, premiered at the Berlin film festival in February 2014 to excellent reviews, winning the Teddy Award for best documentary essay film plus the audience prize. The source timeline files the premiere under 10 January.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: First Bisexual Health Awareness Month declared
description: The Boston-based Bisexual Resource Center declared March 2014 the first Bisexual Health Awareness Month, running a social media campaign on the physical and mental health disparities facing bisexual people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Michael Sam becomes first openly gay player drafted into the NFL
description: The St. Louis Rams selected Michael Sam in the NFL draft, making him the first openly gay player drafted into the National Football League.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marriage equality rally held in Bern on IDAHOT
description: Campaigners gathered on Bern's Münsterplatz on 17 May 2014, the International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia, Interphobia and Transphobia, to demand the opening of marriage to same-sex couples.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Zurich equality prize opened to LGBTI advocacy
description: The City of Zurich's office for gender equality announced on 4 June 2014 that its equality prize would in future also honor work on LGBTI concerns.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Zurich Pride draws a record 12,000 marchers for its 20th edition
description: Marking twenty years of the festival on 14 June 2014, Zurich Pride set an attendance record with 12,000 people joining the demonstration under a defiant motto.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: UN Human Rights Council adopts a traditionalist family resolution
description: On 25 June 2014 the UN Human Rights Council passed a resolution on the protection of the family that held to the conventional heterosexual concept of family, sidelining recognition of diverse family forms.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Second international asexuality conference in Toronto
description: The second International Asexuality Conference was held at Ryerson University in Toronto.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: Executive Order 13672 bars contractor discrimination
description: Executive Order 13672 prohibited United States federal contractors from discriminating on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity. The Grindr timeline dates this to 2016, but the order was signed in 2014.

sources.label: Grindr for Equality — LGBTQ Rights Timeline

title: C1 Financial lists as first US public bank with an openly gay CEO
description: When Florida-based C1 Financial began trading in August 2014, its chief executive Trevor Burgess became the first openly gay CEO of a publicly listed US bank.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Jewish and Moroccan boats join Amsterdam Pride Canal Parade
description: The Amsterdam Pride Canal Parade included its first Jewish boat, with Reform rabbi Marianne van Praag of The Hague aboard, alongside the parade's first Moroccan boat.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: St. Gallen court recognizes two men as legal parents
description: On 26 August 2014 the administrative court of the canton of St. Gallen recognized a male couple as the parents of their child, who had been born through surrogacy in the United States.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Der Kreis opens in Swiss cinemas
description: Stefan Haupt's film about the Zurich journal Der Kreis and the couple at its heart reached Swiss theaters on 18 September 2014.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Switzerland submits Der Kreis for the foreign-language Oscar
description: On 22 September 2014 Switzerland selected Der Kreis as its official entry in the Academy Awards race for best foreign-language film.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Council of States declines to penalize public homophobia
description: On 23 September 2014 the Swiss Council of States voted 22 to 13 against prosecuting publicly expressed homophobia, rejecting a Geneva cantonal initiative to extend the constitution's anti-racism penal norm accordingly.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: UN Human Rights Council passes second resolution on sexual orientation and gender identity
description: The UN Human Rights Council adopted its second resolution addressing sexual orientation and gender identity by a 25-14 vote, the first LGBT-rights resolution backed by a majority of the Council's members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iqaluit holds its first Pride Festival
description: Iqaluit held Nunavut's first Pride Festival.

sources.label: queerevents.ca — Canadian LGBTQ+ History Timeline

title: New South Wales expunges historical homosexual convictions
description: New South Wales passed the Criminal Records Amendment (Historical Homosexual Offences) Act 2014, allowing certain past homosexual offences to be expunged.

sources.label: National Museum of Australia — LGBTI history interactive timeline 1975–2019

title: Supreme Court lets marriage rulings stand in five states
description: The US Supreme Court declined to review five marriage cases, letting lower-court rulings stand and enabling same-sex couples to marry in Utah, Oklahoma, Virginia, Indiana and Wisconsin, with more states to follow.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Palau equalizes the age of consent
description: Palau set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Association queerAltern forms to support older queer people
description: queerAltern, an organization devoted to the concerns of aging LGBT people, was established on 28 October 2014.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Family Circle magazine features a same-sex couple for the first time
description: The November 2014 issue of Family Circle included a same-sex couple, a first for the long-running American family magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maura Healey elected Massachusetts attorney general
description: Maura Healey won election as Attorney General of Massachusetts, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ state attorney general in the United States.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Federal Council proposes opening stepchild adoption
description: On 28 November 2014 the Swiss Federal Council put forward a revision of adoption law intended to let people adopt their partner's children, a change that would cover same-sex couples.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Robbie Rogers wins MLS Cup as first openly gay champion in major US team sports
description: When the LA Galaxy took the Major League Soccer Cup, Robbie Rogers became the first openly gay male athlete to win a major professional team-sports title in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Luzern disbands
description: The Lucerne gay working groups HALU, long the region's central gay organization, dissolved on 10 December 2014.

sources.label: schwulengeschichte.ch — Zeittafel 2010-2019

title: Abby Stein comes out as the first openly transgender woman with ultra-Orthodox ordination
description: Abby Stein came out as transgender, making her the first openly transgender woman ordained by an ultra-Orthodox institution; she had received her rabbinical degree from Yeshiva Viznitz in South Fallsburg, New York, in 2011. She is also the first openly transgender woman raised in a Hasidic community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Aisha Moodie-Mills takes over leadership of the Victory Fund
description: Aisha Moodie-Mills became president and CEO of the Victory Fund — the first woman, first Black woman, and first lesbian to head a leading national LGBT organization in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alberta elects its first openly LGBT legislators
description: Michael Connolly, Ricardo Miranda and Estefania Cortes-Vargas became Alberta's first three openly LGBT members of the Legislative Assembly. Cortes-Vargas later came out as genderqueer, becoming the first out trans person to serve in any Canadian legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alberta protects gender identity and expression from discrimination
description: The Canadian province of Alberta added gender identity and gender expression to its anti-discrimination law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrew Guy becomes Australia's first openly transgender TV host
description: Andrew Guy appeared as a guest presenter on The Project, making him the first openly transgender television host in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentina allows same-sex parents plus biological father on a birth certificate
description: Argentina became the first country in Latin America to record same-sex partners together with a biological parent on a child's birth certificate, listing a lesbian couple, Susana Guichal and Valeria Gaete, alongside sperm donor Hernan Melazzi as parents of their son Antonio.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Audrey Middleton is Big Brother's first openly transgender houseguest
description: Audrey Middleton appeared on the US television show Big Brother as the program's first openly transgender houseguest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austria allows same-sex couples to adopt
description: Austria opened joint adoption to same-sex couples through legislation adopted in 2015.

title: Aydian Dowling is first openly transgender man on a Men's Health cover
description: Aydian Dowling appeared on the cover of a special collector's edition of Men's Health, the first openly transgender man featured on the magazine's cover.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Benjamin Thomas Watt becomes first openly gay professional boxing judge
description: New Zealander Benjamin Thomas Watt became the first openly gay judge in professional boxing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bisexual website Biscuit launches the Purple List

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stacie Gregory becomes Sausalito's first openly lesbian police chief
description: In 2024, Stacie Gregory took over as police chief of Sausalito, California, the first openly LGBTQ+ person and first openly lesbian officer to lead the department.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Thailand passes a marriage equality law
description: Thailand's legislature approved a law opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2024, making it the first country in Southeast Asia to pass marriage equality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK lifts blood donation restrictions for sexually active men
description: Restrictions on sexually active men giving blood were lifted in the UK.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Gabriel Attal becomes first openly gay Prime Minister of France
description: Gabriel Attal was appointed Prime Minister of France on 9 January 2024, the first openly gay person to hold the office and the eighth openly LGBT head of government in Europe.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Puberty blockers withdrawn for under-18s
description: The UK government stopped the routine prescription of puberty blockers to people under 18 following the Cass Review of gender identity services.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Dominica equalizes the age of consent
description: Dominica set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Biden rule expands LGBTQ health care protections
description: The Biden administration announced a rule broadening safeguards against discrimination for gay and transgender people seeking medical care, reversing earlier limits on federal health protections.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Karla Sofía Gascón is first openly trans actor to win a major Cannes prize
description: At the 2024 Cannes Film Festival, Karla Sofía Gascón shared the Best Actress award with her Emilia Pérez costars Selena Gomez, Adriana Paz and Zoe Saldaña, becoming the first openly transgender actor to win a major prize at Cannes.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Namibia equalizes the age of consent
description: Namibia set an equal age of consent regardless of sexual orientation, removing the higher threshold that had applied to same-sex relations.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Worker Protection Act 2023 comes into force
description: The Worker Protection Act 2023 came into force on 26 October 2024, requiring employers to take reasonable steps to prevent the sexual harassment of their employees by third parties.

sources.label: Unite the Union — LGBT+ History Timeline 1967-2024

title: Emily Randall elected first LGBTQ+ Latina in Congress
description: Emily Randall of Washington state was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ Latina member of Congress.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Julie Johnson brings out representation to the US South
description: Julie Johnson of Texas was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly LGBTQ+ member of Congress from the US South.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Sarah McBride elected first transgender US House member
description: Sarah McBride of Delaware was elected to the US House of Representatives, becoming the first openly transgender person elected to the US Congress.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Permanent ban on puberty-suppressing hormones imposed
description: The UK government imposed a permanent ban on the sale and supply of puberty-suppressing hormones for gender-related treatment of minors.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Burkina Faso criminalizes homosexuality
description: In 2025, Burkina Faso adopted legislation making same-sex relations a criminal offence, introducing an explicit ban where none had previously existed.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First gay man elected to the Delaware Senate
description: Delaware elected its first openly gay man to the state senate, adding to LGBTQ+ representation in the legislature. The source does not name the individual.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Gina Ortiz Jones elected first out mayor of San Antonio
description: Gina Ortiz Jones was elected mayor of San Antonio, Texas, becoming the city's first openly LGBTQ+ mayor.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: LGBTQ+ veterans begin receiving compensation
description: LGBTQ+ veterans affected by the former ban on serving in the armed forces began receiving financial compensation following the Etherton Review.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Lithuania's Constitutional Court rules on civil unions for same-sex couples
description: In 2025, Lithuania's Constitutional Court issued a ruling on the legal recognition of same-sex civil unions, adding judicial weight to partnership recognition for same-sex couples in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Poland's last LGBT-free zones are abolished
description: In 2025, the final Polish local governments withdrew their so-called LGBT-free zone declarations, ending the policy that at its peak covered around a third of the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saint Lucia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 2025, consensual same-sex sexual activity ceased to be a criminal offence in Saint Lucia, removing colonial-era penalties from the island's law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Supreme Court defines sex as biological for Equality Act
description: The UK Supreme Court ruled that the terms sex, woman and man in the Equality Act 2010 refer to biological sex assigned at birth, a decision with significant implications for trans people.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Sweden allows legal gender change without restrictions
description: Sweden allowed transgender people to change their legal gender on official documents without medical, surgical, or diagnostic requirements.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: Thailand legalizes adoption by same-sex couples
description: Same-sex couples gained the legal right to jointly adopt children in Thailand.

sources.label: Equaldex — LGBT Rights Timeline

title: The BBC renews its queer dating series I Kissed a Girl
description: The BBC renewed 'I Kissed a Girl' for a second series with Dannii Minogue as host, alongside the continuing 'I Kissed a Boy', reality dating shows centred on LGBT contestants.

sources.label: BBC — LGBTQ+ Timeline (History of the BBC)

title: Transgender candidate elected to Ohio city council
description: An openly transgender candidate was elected to a city council in Ohio, a local milestone for trans representation. The source does not name the individual or city.

sources.label: Pride & Progress — Decades timeline

title: Trinidad and Tobago re-criminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 2025, an appellate ruling in Trinidad and Tobago restored the criminal status of consensual same-sex acts, reversing the 2018 High Court decision that had struck the ban down.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sarah McBride becomes first openly transgender member of the US House
description: Sarah McBride of Delaware was sworn into the US House of Representatives in January 2025, the first openly transgender person to serve in the chamber.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trump order restricts gender markers on passports
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order rolling back gender identity designations, and the State Department suspended issuing passports with an X marker; in November 2025 the Supreme Court allowed passport sex markers to be required to match biological sex.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump orders ban on transgender service members
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order barring transgender people from military service; after courts intervened, the Supreme Court in May 2025 allowed the administration to enforce the ban.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump order targets gender-affirming care for youth
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order ending federal support for institutions that provide gender-affirming care to transgender youth; a federal judge blocked the order.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Trump order restricts trans women in sports
description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order titled "Keeping Men Out of Women’s Sports" aimed at barring transgender women from women’s athletic competition.

sources.label: CNN — LGBTQ Rights Milestones Fast Facts

title: Lithuania recognizes same-sex civil unions
description: Lithuania began legally recognizing same-sex couples through civil unions or registered partnerships, extending many of the rights of marriage.

description: Austria allowed transgender people to change their legal gender on official documents without medical, surgical, or diagnostic requirements.

title: CBeebies introduces a children's show with a two-dad family
description: The animated series 'Rafi the Wishing Wizard' launched on CBeebies, following a young wizard who lives in Manchester with her two dads, presenting an LGBT-parent family in mainstream children's programming.

title: Erica Deuso sworn in as first transgender mayor
description: Erica Deuso was sworn in as mayor in Pennsylvania, reported as the state's first openly transgender mayor. Details of the recent event warrant review.

title: Niger criminalizes homosexuality
description: In 2026, Niger introduced criminal penalties for same-sex relations, adding an explicit ban to its law where none had previously existed.

title: Rob Jetten becomes first openly gay Prime Minister of the Netherlands
description: In 2026, Rob Jetten took office as Prime Minister of the Netherlands, the first openly gay person to lead a Dutch government and the ninth openly LGBT head of government in Europe.
